title: Watertown seeks new city attorney after Syracuse law firm steps away
summary: Watertown (NY) is replacing its city attorney after Syracuse-based law firm Bond, Schoeneck & King withdrew from the role following criticism of attorney Kristen Smith's handling of ethics complaints and a state police investigation into a $3.34M golf course purchase. The city plans to issue an RFP within 30 days and appoint a new firm by end of 2026, preferring a local law firm for the general counsel position while BSK continues litigation and specialty work.

political_signal.description: Council member dissatisfaction with city attorney's handling of ethics complaints, legal bills redaction practices, and involvement in state police investigation; third attorney search in two years indicates governance instability.

processing_notes: Dollar amount '$3.4 million' and '$3.34 million' refer to same Watertown Golf Club purchase transaction
Article indicates intergovernmental coordination with state police and state attorney general's office
Third leadership change in this role within 24 months suggests systemic governance challenge
No direct Syracuse CIO department impact, but governance/procurement process methodology may be relevant
